summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Boston Fed touts FedNow
articles.description: The Federal Reserve has been updating its instant payments service, FedNow, to attract more banks and ultimately reach more consumers.

articles.title: Pilot, NCR Voyix agree to 5-year pact
articles.description: The retailer, which has used NCR’s self-checkout kiosks for years, will now also deploy the payment service company’s point-of-sale technology.

articles.title: Affirm pursues affluent consumers
articles.description: Shoppers with more purchasing power spend more, and are more likely to use the company’s products again, an executive said.

articles.title: Why EWA services matter
articles.description: “We’re already seeing high-growth from restaurant and hospitality companies that now view instant payments, including digital tips, as a competitive advantage,” writes one earned wage access CEO.

articles.title: Visa, Mastercard jockey to set agentic standards
articles.description: The card networks are locking arms with big tech players and digital payments to shape the evolving agentic commerce ecosystem.

articles.title: Capital One cuts Discover employees
articles.description: Numbers shared with the state of Illinois indicate 1,075 people will be let go in May, and another 81 on June 1.

articles.title: SoFi’s stablecoin joins Mastercard’s network
articles.description: The card company is fulfilling its ambitions to bring more stablecoins into its payment network.

articles.title: Inside a startup building tools for AI agents
articles.description: Funded by Stripe and Samsung, Circuit & Chisel wants to set the protocol for how artificial intelligence agents navigate the web autonomously.
